name: Gwangju (South Korea)

colors.symbolism: Mudeungsan, the sacred mountain that stands east of the city
purity and peace, recalling Gwangju's role in Korea's democracy movement
the warmth of the rising sun and the spirit of Gwangju's citizens

description: A green field charged at the centre with a white stylised emblem in the shape of a flying dove or radiant sun above abstract waves, with a red accent representing the rising sun and the name of the city in white.
history: Gwangju, a Metropolitan City (Gwangyeoksi) since 1995, adopted its current flag in May 1995 along with promotion to special-city status. The emblem is an abstract design symbolising Mudeungsan mountain, the surrounding fields and the bright sun, and recalls Gwangju's identity as a 'city of light' (its name 광주/光州 literally means 'light province/city') and a centre of Korea's pro-democracy movement.

features.symbolism: Mudeungsan, the sacred mountain that stands east of the city
abstract dove- or sun-and-wave motif, evoking the city's name 'light province' (光州) and its role as a centre of Korea's democratic movement, with the red accent representing the rising sun
